REQ-ALLOC-001.summary: Provide portman alloc <service-name> to allocate the next available port for a service

REQ-ALLOC-002.summary: Support requesting a specific port with --port flag; fail if already allocated

REQ-ALLOC-003.summary: Support requesting a port within a range with --range flag; allocate lowest available

REQ-ALLOC-004.summary: Support allocating multiple ports at once with --count flag

REQ-ALLOC-005.summary: Support port type/category hints with --type flag to pick from appropriate default range

REQ-ALLOC-006.summary: Record every allocation with port, protocol, service, project, category, timestamp, allocated_by, and description

REQ-ALLOC-007.summary: Validate that requested port is not in use by another process (live check) before confirming allocation

REQ-ALLOC-008.summary: Return allocation results in human-readable and machine-readable formats (--format json|table|plain)

REQ-CLI-001.summary: CLI entry point is portman, installed via pyproject.toml scripts section

REQ-CLI-002.summary: Top-level commands: init, scan, alloc, release, reserve, list, info, search, conflicts, check, gc, export, import, config, serve

REQ-CLI-003.summary: Use typer for CLI framework with type-hint-driven commands

REQ-CLI-004.summary: Support --verbose / -v flag globally for debug output

REQ-CLI-005.summary: Support --quiet / -q flag globally for minimal output suitable for scripting

REQ-CLI-006.summary: All destructive commands require confirmation unless --yes / -y is passed

REQ-CLI-007.summary: Provide shell completion support for bash, zsh, fish via --install-completion

REQ-CONF-001.summary: Before any allocation, check registry for existing assignments on same port+protocol

REQ-CONF-002.summary: Before any allocation, perform live port probe to detect if port is bound by any process

REQ-CONF-003.summary: Provide portman conflicts to report all current conflicts (registry dupes, registry vs live, cross-project)

REQ-CONF-004.summary: On portman scan, automatically flag and highlight conflicts found across scanned projects

REQ-CONF-005.summary: Suggest alternative ports when conflict detected, picking nearest available in same category range

REQ-DMN-001.summary: Provide portman daemon start (foreground) and portman daemon install (systemd user service)

REQ-DMN-002.summary: Daemon exposes Unix domain socket at ~/.portman/portmand.sock for local IPC; CLI/SDK use daemon when running, fallback to direct DB

REQ-DMN-003.summary: Daemon exposes HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:9800 mirroring REST endpoints for language-agnostic access

REQ-DMN-004.summary: Daemon accepts allocation requests from services at startup; primary runtime allocation path

REQ-DMN-005.summary: Daemon performs periodic health checks (default 60s) probing all registered ports, updating status

REQ-DMN-006.summary: Daemon emits structured log events to ~/.portman/portmand.log with rotation (10MB, 3 backups)

REQ-DMN-007.summary: Provide portman daemon stop and portman daemon status commands

REQ-DMN-008.summary: Provide portman daemon uninstall to remove systemd service

REQ-DMN-009.summary: Daemon performs initial full scan on startup if registry is empty

REQ-DMN-010.summary: Daemon handles concurrent requests safely using async I/O; no duplicate port allocations

REQ-INT-001.summary: Provide helper script/shell function for services to call at startup for port allocation

REQ-INT-002.summary: Provide Python SDK / importable module for programmatic port allocation

REQ-INT-003.summary: Support .env file generation with portman env <service-name>

REQ-INT-004.summary: Support .portman.yaml manifest for declaring port requirements and bulk allocation

REQ-INT-005.summary: Provide curl-friendly allocation endpoint for non-Python services via daemon HTTP API

REQ-LIFE-001.summary: Provide portman release <service-name> to release all ports allocated to a service

REQ-LIFE-002.summary: Provide portman release --port <N> to release a specific port

REQ-LIFE-003.summary: Support port reservations with portman reserve <port> --for <service> --until <date>

REQ-LIFE-004.summary: Provide portman check to validate registry against live state; report stale and rogue ports

REQ-LIFE-005.summary: Provide portman gc to interactively clean up stale allocations

REQ-NFR-001.summary: Port scanning of typical project directory (10k files) completes in under 10 seconds

REQ-NFR-003.summary: Web dashboard loads in under 2 seconds with up to 1000 port allocations

REQ-NFR-004.summary: SQLite registry must use WAL mode for concurrent CLI and web dashboard access

REQ-NFR-005.summary: All registry mutations must be transactional — no partial updates on failure

REQ-NFR-006.summary: Scanner must handle permission errors gracefully (skip unreadable files with warning)

REQ-NFR-007.summary: CLI output uses color and formatting via rich for readability

REQ-NFR-009.summary: Web dashboard usable without JavaScript (server-side rendered tables as baseline)

REQ-NFR-010.summary: Web dashboard binds to 127.0.0.1 by default; require --host 0.0.0.0 for external exposure

REQ-NFR-011.summary: Registry file permissions set to user-only read/write (0600)

REQ-NFR-012.summary: Must run on Linux and macOS; Windows is non-goal for v1

REQ-NFR-013.summary: Use only Python stdlib + listed dependencies; no system packages beyond Python 3.12+

REQ-POL-001.summary: Define configurable port range policies per category in ~/.portman/config.yaml

REQ-POL-002.summary: Enforce allocations with category hints fall within configured range; warn with --force override

REQ-POL-003.summary: Allow defining reserved/blacklisted port ranges that are never allocated

REQ-POL-004.summary: Allow defining per-project overrides for port ranges in .portman.yaml

REQ-REG-001.summary: Store port registry in SQLite database at configurable location (default ~/.portman/registry.db)

REQ-REG-002.summary: Provide portman list to display all registered ports in sortable table

REQ-REG-003.summary: Provide portman list --service <name> to filter by service

REQ-REG-004.summary: Provide portman list --range <start>-<end> to filter by port range

REQ-REG-005.summary: Provide portman list --category <cat> to filter by category

REQ-REG-006.summary: Provide portman list --status active|stale|reserved|conflict to filter by status

REQ-REG-007.summary: Provide portman info <port> to show detailed port allocation information

REQ-REG-008.summary: Provide portman search <query> to fuzzy-search across service names, descriptions, and paths

REQ-REG-009.summary: Support exporting registry in json, csv, or yaml format

REQ-REG-010.summary: Support importing allocations from file for bootstrapping

REQ-SCAN-001.summary: On portman init, recursively scan project root directories for port assignments in known configuration files

REQ-SCAN-002.summary: Parse docker-compose, .env, package.json, Python files, nginx conf, Procfile, YAML/TOML config files for port numbers

REQ-SCAN-003.summary: Detect ports from currently running processes on the host using ss/lsof/netstat

REQ-SCAN-004.summary: For each discovered port, record: port number, protocol, service name, source file path, line number, and whether in use

REQ-SCAN-005.summary: Classify discovered ports into categories: http, database, messaging, grpc, custom

REQ-SCAN-006.summary: After scanning, present summary and prompt user to confirm before committing to registry; highlight conflicts

REQ-SCAN-007.summary: Support incremental re-scan (portman scan) that detects new/changed/removed assignments and shows diff

REQ-SCAN-008.summary: Allow users to specify directories to exclude from scanning

REQ-SCAN-009.summary: Scan installed software for port assignments: systemd units, system service configs, package manifests, Docker containers, K3s/K8s services

REQ-SCAN-010.summary: For installed software ports, record discovery source, default port, and whether configurable or hardcoded

REQ-SCAN-011.summary: On portman init, scan both project directories and installed system software; support --projects-only and --system-only flags

REQ-SCAN-012.summary: Maintain built-in knowledge base of well-known service ports to enrich discovered ports with service names

REQ-TST-PM-001.summary: Unit tests for each parser (docker-compose, .env, nginx, etc.) with sample fixtures

REQ-TST-PM-002.summary: Unit tests for allocator: sequential allocation, specific port, range allocation, conflict rejection

REQ-TST-PM-003.summary: Unit tests for validator: registry conflicts, live port detection, stale detection

REQ-TST-PM-004.summary: Integration tests for CLI commands using typer.testing.CliRunner

REQ-TST-PM-005.summary: Integration tests for REST API using TestClient

REQ-TST-PM-006.summary: Test fixtures with mock project directories containing various config file types

REQ-TST-PM-007.summary: All tests use isolated in-memory or temp-dir SQLite database, never user's real registry

REQ-TST-PM-008.summary: Unit tests for system parsers (systemd units, docker inspect, k8s services) with mock command output fixtures

REQ-TST-PM-009.summary: Integration tests for daemon (start, allocate via socket, concurrent allocations, stop) with temporary socket path

REQ-TST-PM-010.summary: Unit tests for well-known ports knowledge base covering port-to-service resolution

REQ-WEB-001.summary: Serve web dashboard via portman serve, defaulting to highest allocated HTTP port + 1 or 9999

REQ-WEB-002.summary: Allow overriding dashboard port with --port flag

REQ-WEB-003.summary: Dashboard home shows total ports by category, sortable/filterable table, conflicts in red, range utilization bars

REQ-WEB-004.summary: Provide Port Map visualization showing allocated vs available ports as heatmap or block grid

REQ-WEB-005.summary: Provide service detail view with all port allocations, source files, and history

REQ-WEB-006.summary: Provide live status page that probes allocated ports and shows listening/not responding/conflicting

REQ-WEB-007.summary: Support allocating and releasing ports from the web UI

REQ-WEB-008.summary: Use FastAPI backend and Jinja2 templates or lightweight JS frontend; no heavy SPA framework

REQ-WEB-009.summary: Dashboard auto-registers its own port in registry as portman-dashboard with category http

REQ-WEB-010.summary: Provide REST API at /api/v1/ for programmatic access to all port management operations
